blocks.text: Closing Thought for Week 12 — and for Your Whole Journey
Always remember: Discipleship is not a 12-week program. It is BECOMING. It is SPIRITUAL FORMATION. It is a lifelong apprenticeship to Jesus. This curriculum was the beginning — not the destination.
Jesus is not done forming you. He will keep shaping your heart: through His Spirit, through His Word, through your practices, through your daily walk, through community, through grace.
You have entered the narrow way. You are becoming the good tree. You are building on the rock. Your life will stand. Because Jesus Himself is your foundation. ALLELUIA!
Appendices
Discipleship & Apprenticeship Covenant
blocks.subtitle: For Participants on the 12-Week Sermon on the Mount Journey

blocks.text: Following Jesus is not simply learning information — it is entering into a life of apprenticeship to Jesus. This covenant expresses the heart posture of a disciple who desires to practice the teachings of Jesus, not merely hear them. It reflects the themes and practices of the 12-week curriculum and serves as a commitment to becoming the kind of person Jesus is forming you to be. This covenant is made not out of pressure, performance, or perfectionism — but out of love, desire, and the longing to walk closely with Jesus.
My Discipleship & Apprenticeship to Jesus Covenant
1. A Covenant of the Heart
I commit to walk through this journey with a heart open to Jesus’ transforming work. I recognize discipleship is about who I am becoming more than what I am doing.

items: Allow Jesus to form my inner life.
Welcome correction, healing, and growth.
Walk in humility, honesty, and surrender.

blocks.text: “Make the tree good and its fruit will be good.”
2. A Covenant of Practice
I understand that Jesus changes me as I practice His teachings. I commit to take each weekly practice seriously, trusting that Christ meets me in the doing.

items: Practice Jesus’ teachings daily.
Participate fully in weekly exercises.
Take small, consistent steps of obedience.

blocks.text: “Everyone who hears these words of Mine and puts them into practice…”
3. A Covenant of Purity & Inner Wholeness
I commit to allow Jesus to purify my heart — my thoughts, desires, emotions, and motives.

items: Guard my heart from anger, contempt, and lust.
Pursue reconciliation quickly.
Remove anything that weakens my purity or integrity.

blocks.text: “Blessed are the pure in heart, for they shall see God.”
4. A Covenant of Speech & Integrity
I commit to speak truthfully, gently, and clearly. I want my words to match my heart.

items: Let my “Yes” be Yes and my “No” be No.
Avoid exaggeration, deception, or manipulation.
Use my words to build and bless, not tear down.

blocks.text: “Out of the abundance of the heart the mouth speaks.”
5. A Covenant of Meekness, Generosity & Non-Retaliation
I commit to letting Jesus shape how I respond to provocation, injustice, or inconvenience.

items: Respond gently when wronged.
Go the second mile when needed.
Give freely to those in need.

blocks.text: “Do not resist an evil person… go with him two miles.”
6. A Covenant of Love & Mercy
I commit, with God’s help, to love even when it is hard, and to bless those who oppose me.

items: Pray for my enemies.
Release bitterness and forgive freely.
Respond to hostility with mercy.

blocks.text: “Love your enemies and pray for those who persecute you.”
7. A Covenant of Secret Devotion
I commit to develop a hidden life with God — unseen by others and rooted in intimacy.

items: Give in secret.
Pray in secret.
Fast in secret.

blocks.text: “Your Father who sees in secret will reward you.”
8. A Covenant of Forgiveness & Emotional Freedom
I commit to walk in mercy, letting Jesus free my heart from bitterness, wounds, and offense.

items: Practice forgiveness regularly.
Bless those who have hurt me.
Release the weight of past pain.

blocks.text: “Forgive, and you will be forgiven.”
9. A Covenant of Trust & Kingdom Priority
I commit to place God’s Kingdom above my fears, worries, and anxieties.

items: Seek first the Kingdom.
Release daily worry to God.
Trust the Father’s care.

blocks.text: “Your Father knows what you need.”
10. A Covenant of Humility, Discernment & Healthy Boundaries
I commit to see myself clearly, correct others gently, and live with wise boundaries.

items: Remove the plank from my own eye.
Judge not with condemnation.
Guard what is holy and set safe boundaries.

blocks.text: “First remove the plank… then you will see clearly.”
11. A Covenant of Persistent Faith
I commit to ask boldly, seek passionately, and knock persistently.

items: Pray with expectation.
Return to prayer when discouraged.
Trust the Father’s goodness.

blocks.text: “Ask… seek… knock.”
12. A Covenant of the Narrow Way & Christlike Living
I commit to enter through the narrow gate and build my life on the teachings of Jesus.

items: Choose the narrow way daily.
Become the good tree that bears good fruit.
Build my life on the rock of obedience.

blocks.text: “Enter through the narrow gate.”
“The wise man built his house on the rock.”
My Commitment
By signing this covenant, I am not promising perfection. I am presenting my heart to Jesus for transformation.

blocks.title: I am committing to:
options: Walk through this journey with sincerity and desire.
Allow Jesus to form His life within me.
Continue this apprenticeship beyond the 12 weeks.

blocks.text: Lord Jesus, I thank You for walking with me over these past 12 weeks. Thank You for teaching me, shaping me, correcting me, and healing me. I am grateful for Your transforming work in my life, making me more like You in every way. I acknowledge that I could not change myself, but through Your help and by practicing Your commands, You have renewed my heart as I have journeyed with You. Thank You for guiding my thoughts, desires, reactions, and character, and for forming Your life within me. I praise You for Your faithfulness and love. In Jesus’ Name, Amen.
blocks.title: Thanksgiving Prayer

blocks.text: Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)
blocks.subtitle: Honest questions. Simple answers. Real help for the journey.

blocks.text: 1. What if I fail a lot while trying to follow Jesus?

You will. Everyone does. Jesus is not surprised, disappointed, or angry when you stumble. He helps you back up and teaches you to walk again. Remember: God looks at who you are becoming, not how many times you fall. See: Proverbs 24:16, 1 John 1:9
2. What if I don’t feel anything when I pray or read the Bible?
Feelings come and go. Jesus is with you even when you feel nothing. Keep showing up. God often works the most when we don’t feel it. See: Matthew 28:20, Psalm 46:10
3. What if I find some of Jesus’ teachings hard?
You’re not alone. His disciples also struggled. Hard teachings usually mean big growth is coming. Ask Jesus for help. He shapes your heart to make His way possible. See: John 6:60, Philippians 4:13
4. How do I know if I’m really growing?

There are several signs that confirm your spiritual growth. You know you are growing when you notice yourself reacting differently than you did before, your heart becomes softer, and you find yourself apologizing more quickly. You begin to desire what is good, resist things that could harm you, love others more deeply, and place your trust in God sooner than you used to. Growth is often slow and gentle — like a tree. So be patient with yourself. See: Mark 4:26–28, John 15:1–5
5. What should I do when I feel spiritually dry?
Stay close to Jesus even if you feel empty. Dry seasons are normal. Keep reading, keep praying, keep coming to group. Rain always comes. God never wastes dryness. See: Psalm 63:1, Isaiah 44:3
6. What if some sins keep repeating in my life?
Jesus does not give up on you. He walks with you step by step. Confess quickly, talk to a trusted believer, and practice the weekly exercises. Small steps break big chains. See: James 5:16, John 8:36
7. Why do I need community? Can’t I just follow Jesus alone?
No one follows Jesus alone — even Jesus walked with friends. We grow faster, safer, and deeper with others. Community helps you stay encouraged and honest. See: Hebrews 10:24–25, Acts 2:42
8. What if I feel unworthy or too broken to be a disciple?
Jesus chooses the broken, the weak, and the ordinary. Your weakness is the doorway to His strength. He wants you, all of you, right now, as you are. See: 2 Corinthians 12:9, Psalm 147:3
9. How do I forgive someone who hurt me deeply?

Forgiveness is a process, not a moment. Start by giving the pain to God. Ask Jesus to help you take small steps toward letting go. Forgiveness does not mean the hurt was okay — it means you’re letting God heal you. See: Matthew 6:14–15, Ephesians 4:32
10. Why does Jesus ask us to love our enemies? Isn’t that too much?
It is too much — for our old selves. But Jesus gives new hearts. Loving enemies frees you from anger, bitterness, and revenge. It is not weakness. It is real strength. See: Matthew 5:44, Romans 12:21
11. What if I’m afraid God will give up on me?
He won’t. God finishes what He starts. He is more committed to your growth than you are. See: Philippians 1:6, John 10:28–29
12. How long will it take for me to change?

Change is slow, steady, and beautiful. Some things change fast. Other things take years. God is not in a hurry — but He is always working. Think tree, not microwave. See: Galatians 5:22–23, 2 Corinthians 3:18
13. Is God disappointed with me when I worry or doubt?
No. God understands your fear and meets you with compassion. Worry and doubt are invitations to trust Him more, not reasons for shame. See: Matthew 6:32–33, Mark 9:24
14. How do I stay strong after this 12-week course ends?

To stay strong after this 12-week course ends, keep practicing important habits like reading Scripture, praying, spending time with a supportive community, worshiping, obeying God, serving others, and being generous. These small daily actions will help you stay rooted in Jesus and continue growing in your faith. See: Acts 2:42–47, Colossians 2:6–7
15. What if I feel tempted to go back to my old life?

Temptation is normal. The fact that you even care shows you have a new heart. Call a friend, pray, remember who you are in Christ, and take the small step Jesus is showing you. See: 1 Corinthians 10:13, Galatians 5:16
16. How do I hear God’s voice?

Through Scripture first and most. Through peace in your heart. Through wise believers. Through gentle nudges from the Holy Spirit within you. Through circumstances that open or close doors. If you are seeking Jesus, He knows how to speak so you can understand. See: John 10:27, Psalm 119:105
Meeting With Jesus Daily
blocks.subtitle: A Simple Guide For Beginning and Growing a Daily Life With God

blocks.text: Why Meet with Jesus Daily?

items: Because following Jesus is a relationship, not a theory.
Because your soul needs His voice more than your phone’s voice.
Because His presence makes the tree good from the inside out.
Jesus Himself often withdrew to meet with the Father (Luke 5:16).
If the Son of God needed daily connection, we certainly do.

blocks.text: This guide will help you develop a simple, life-giving rhythm that keeps your heart open and your life rooted in Him.
A Simple Daily Rhythm
Below is a 10–20-minute pattern anyone can do — beginner or mature believer. Think of it as a gentle, relational meeting with Jesus.
1. Pause & Settle (1 minute)
Sit quietly. Breathe deeply. Let your heart slow down. Pray: “Jesus, here I am. I open my heart to You.” Even 20 seconds of stillness changes everything.
2. Read the Word (5 minutes)

Pick a short passage — one paragraph is enough. Preferably from the Gospels, Psalms, or the BECOME® reading for the week. Read slowly. Read again if needed. Ask: What is Jesus saying? What stands out? What do I feel stirred by? Don’t rush. Don’t analyze deeply. Just listen.
3. Talk with Jesus (5 minutes)

Be honest. Be simple. Be yourself. Share your heart: your fears, your joys, your frustrations, your hopes, your temptations, your dreams, your weakness. Then pray the week’s formation prayer (from the workbook). This makes daily prayer deeply connected to the Jesus Way.
4. Listen in Silence (2 minutes)
Sit still again. Let Jesus speak — not always in words but in: peace, clarity, conviction, rest, quiet joy. Listening is where formation happens.
5. Respond & Obey (1 minute)

End by asking: “Jesus, what do You want me to DO today?” It may be: forgive someone; be quiet instead of reacting; bless someone; apologize humbly; let something go; remove a temptation; give something; pray for someone; trust instead of worrying. Write it down. Ask Jesus’ help. Do it. Because transformation happens in the doing, with Jesus’ help.
Bonus: Morning & Evening Breath Prayers
These are short, powerful prayers you can pray anytime during your day.

items: Morning: “Jesus, make the tree good.” (Matthew 12:33)
Midday: “Your Kingdom come in me.” (Matthew 6:10)
Evening: “Lord, I give everyone and everything to You.” (Dallas Willard)

blocks.text: These 8-second prayers anchor your heart in grace all day long.
Tips for a Consistent Daily Practice

items: Keep it simple — Don’t try to do too much. Just be real with Jesus.
Be consistent, not perfect — Daily is ideal, but missing a day doesn’t break anything. Start again the next day.
Pick a place — A chair. A corner. A space in your room. Let your body learn the rhythm.
Use Scripture, not random inspiration — Jesus forms us most deeply through His words.
Bring your whole heart — Don’t hide. He already knows. He is already smiling at you.

blocks.text: A Simple Model You Can Remember — SPLO
Easy, relational, powerful:

items: S → Scripture – Read the Word.
P → Prayer – Talk honestly with Jesus.
L → Listen – Sit in silence. Receive His peace.
O → Obey – Do the one thing He showed you.

blocks.text: The Goal
Not finishing a reading plan. Not “having devotions.” Not spiritual performance.
The goal is: To walk with Jesus daily so you can become like Jesus deeply and live like Jesus freely. That’s how a disciple grows.
